macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Oil Prices Jump Amid Middle East Tensions, Fueling Inflation Fears
items.summary: Oil prices surged earlier in the session due to escalating Middle East conflict, raising concerns over global inflation; RBA minutes indicate potential inflation reaching 5% in Q2 if oil stays near USD 100, with markets sliding 7.8% in March.

items.title: Stock Market Rejoins 'Iran War Trade' as Earnings Tailwind Fades
items.summary: S&P 500 stalled after mega-cap tech earnings, with crude oil, Treasury yields, and dollar stuck in war trade mode; technical indicators show weakening conviction amid shrinking volumes and negative RSI divergence.

items.title: President Trump Warns of Strikes on Iran's Oil Infrastructure
items.summary: Trump warned the US could target Iran’s electric plants, oil wells, and Kharg Island if no deal is reached and the Strait is not reopened, intensifying geopolitical risks.

items.title: ISM Manufacturing PMI at 52.7, Services Data Awaited
items.summary: April ISM manufacturing PMI matched March at 52.7 with surging prices, shrinking employment, and softening orders; upcoming ISM services PMI and RBA rate hike could amplify hawkish central bank signals.

items.title: Household Spending Growth Eases, Fixed Investments Contract
items.summary: Household spending growth slowed to 0.5% from 1.0% in Q3, while fixed investments fell -1.9% due to declines in housing and IP rights, though machinery investments recovered.

items.title: South Korean Won Drops 1.24%, Rupiah Heads for Monthly Loss
items.summary: South Korean Won fell 1.24% amid FX updates; Indonesian Rupiah set for monthly loss due to strong dollar and fiscal concerns.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $80K for First Time Since January Amid Bullish Momentum
items.summary: Bitcoin bulls have pushed the price above $80,300, flipping MACD bullish after a downtrend bottom at $58K, signaling a relief rally with potential to reach $86K.

items.title: Bitcoin Breakout Above $80K Could Trigger Major Altcoin Rally
items.summary: Bitcoin's official break above $80,000 is seen as a market shift, with analysts eyeing $86K next and strength in altcoins like Avac, Pepe, and Sooie breaking key trends.

items.title: XRP Supply Shock Confirmed as Banks Accumulate Amid 62% Drawdown
items.summary: Institutional giants like Goldman Sachs hold $153.8M in XRP per 13F filings, with massive transfers to cold storage by large holders and 82% volume surge despite price stagnation.

items.title: DTCC Plans Tokenized Securities Platform with July Pilot and October Launch
items.summary: Wall Street's DTCC is launching a tokenized securities platform, boosting on-chain trading and DeFi, alongside Western Union launching USDPT stablecoin on Solana.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Strong at $80K Despite Macro Pressures and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Real spot demand, ETF inflows, and large buyers support Bitcoin's push toward breakout, though it tests a major supply zone where past rallies failed.

items.title: Altcoins Like Zcash, TON Show Breakouts as DTCC Advances Tokenization
items.summary: Zcash breaks out alongside other altcoins, while DTCC's tokenized stock service and potential on-chain trading developments signal major blockchain adoption.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Clash of Clans Adds 20 Golden Dragons to Every Army for 24 Hours, Breaking the Game
items.summary: Clash of Clans temporarily added 20 Golden Dragons, with 32,000 hitpoints and 1,400 DPS each, to every player's army for 24 hours, making bases defenseless.

items.title: Take-Two CEO Describes GTA 6 Expectations as 'Terrifying'; Bank of America Suggests $80 Price
items.summary: Take-Two's CEO expressed terror over GTA 6 hype, while Bank of America argues Rockstar should raise the price to $80 for the industry's benefit; game to launch on PS5 and Xbox first.
items.source: Kinda Funny Games Daily / Bloomberg & VGC

items.title: Halo Studios Reportedly Developing Remakes of Halo 2 and Halo 3
items.summary: Claims indicate Halo Studios is actively working on remakes of Halo 2 and Halo 3 as part of Microsoft's Xbox platform changes.

items.title: Warhorse Studios Teases New Lord of the Rings Game in Unreal Engine 5
items.summary: New details and teases emerged for Warhorse Studios' upcoming Lord of the Rings game, following reports of a project in development.
items.source: MathChief - The Best of Gaming! / Insider Gaming

items.title: The Division 2 Launches Triple XP Week, New Global Event, and Expensive Bundles
items.summary: The Division 2 announces a week of triple XP, a new global event, and premium bundles to boost player progression and engagement.

items.title: Fighting Game News Roundup: Tekken 8 Kunimitsu Trailer, KOF XV Update, Guilty Gear Patch
items.summary: Recent reveals include Tekken 8's Kunimitsu trailer, King of Fighters XV update, Mr. Karate in City of the Wolves, and upcoming Guilty Gear Strive bugfix patch.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: AMD Reports Q1 Revenue Up 38% YoY to $10.25B, Beats Estimates; Q2 Forecast Above Expectations
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices reported first-quarter revenue of $10.25 billion, up 38% year-over-year and surpassing the $9.89 billion estimate, driven by 57% growth in Data Center revenue to $5.8 billion; the company forecasts second-quarter revenue above Wall Street expectations, sending shares up over 7% after hours.

items.title: Apple Held Exploratory Talks with Intel and Visited Samsung Plant to Explore US Chip Production
items.summary: Apple has engaged in early-stage discussions with Intel and executives visited a Samsung facility in Texas to consider manufacturing core chips for its devices domestically.

items.title: Intel Stock Surges 12% to All-Time High on Report of Apple Chip Production Talks
items.summary: Intel's shares jumped over 12% to a new all-time high following reports that Apple is in exploratory talks with the company to produce chips for Apple's devices in the US.
